description: Reorganize repository structure: move ML content to ml/ subdirectory, create new directory structure for v4.0 architecture, update .gitignore for Next.js
steps: Create ml/ directory and move notebooks/, data/, models/, scripts/ into ml/ (e.g., ml/notebooks/, ml/data/, ml/models/, ml/scripts/)
Create the following empty directories: frontend/, api/, docs/assets/
Move existing docs/*.png files to docs/assets/
Create api/README.md with placeholder content explaining this will contain Azure Functions backend
Update .gitignore to add Next.js specific ignores (node_modules/, .next/, out/, .env.local) while preserving existing Python ignores
Remove the old index.html file (will be replaced by Next.js landing page)
Keep the existing .github/workflows/ directory (will be updated in FEAT-004)
acceptance_criteria: ml/data/ contains all CSV files and knowledge_base/
docs/assets/ contains all .png files from docs/
.gitignore includes both Python and Next.js ignores
No data is lost in the reorganization
verification: Run: ls ml/notebooks/ && ls ml/data/ && ls ml/models/ to confirm files were moved
Run: ls docs/assets/ to confirm PNGs moved
Run: cat .gitignore and verify it has both Python and Next.js sections
findings: All moves done via git mv preserving history. CSV files in data/ are gitignored by *.csv pattern but were already tracked so remain in git. Root __pycache__ and scripts/__pycache__ were not tracked (already in .gitignore) so just removed from working tree. The .gitignore was reorganized with separate PYTHON and NODE.JS/NEXT.JS sections. node_modules/ was already in the old .gitignore under Azure section, moved to the Next.js section for clarity.
